content.text:
You are a helpful coding assistant. Be concise.

Workspace map:
- The project repo is mounted at the burrow workspace root.
- /workspace/.canopy/agent.json is the rendered agent definition (warren seeded it).
- /workspace/.mulch/expertise/<domain>.jsonl holds the project's expertise records.
- /workspace/.seeds/issues.jsonl holds the project's issue queue.

Operating contract:
- Edit files in place. Run tests when relevant.
- Quality gates are terminal, not advisory. You are NOT done until the gate exits zero. Resolve the command in this order: `$WARREN_QUALITY_GATE` if set, otherwise the command documented in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md, otherwise fall back to `bun run check:all` or `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test`. Run it before committing and again before reporting completion. Do not declare the task complete, hand off, or end the session with a red gate — fix failures (including lint warnings, which CI treats as errors) until it is green. If the gate is genuinely unfixable in this run, say so explicitly and leave the work open rather than claiming success.
- Use git as you normally would. Commit your changes; warren reaps the branch and pushes upstream.
- Committing is mandatory, not the same as staging. `git add` ALONE IS NOT ENOUGH — you must run `git commit` so the work lands as a real commit. A run that ends with staged-but-uncommitted changes is treated as a FAILURE (`dropped_commit`), not a success. Before you report completion, run `git status`/`git log` and confirm your changes are in a commit, not just staged. The only exception is when you have genuinely made no file changes at all.
- Do not run `git push` yourself — warren handles the push host-side after the run terminates.

---

content.text:
pl-ee1e  approved  rev 1
Name:     nightwatch patrol 2026-05-30 fixes
Seed:     seeds-2c5b
Template: refactor
Created:  2026-05-30T11:06:12.067Z
Updated:  2026-05-30T11:06:12.067Z
Review suggested (no reviewer recorded yet)

Sections:
  context
    Automated nightwatch patrol on 2026-05-30 surfaced four small quality issues in the seeds repo: (1) a real bug where `sd update --title` stores the user's title without trimming whitespace, while `sd create --title` already trims — same field, two storage shapes; (2) a JSON/non-JSON behavior mismatch in `sd upgrade --check` where the non-JSON path sets `process.exitCode = 1` when an update is available but the --json path does not; (3) dead code — `bunx knip` flags three unused exports (`writeConfig`, `isFormatMode`, `formatExtensionsLine`) and four unused exported types (`DomainSource`, `SectionRequest`, `PlanOutcome`, `ConvoyStatus`) that no caller imports; (4) configuration drift — `knip.json` emits 7 hints about stale ignore entries (dist/**, coverage/**, test-results/**, .seeds/**), no-match `extensions/**/*.ts` patterns, and a redundant `src/index.ts` entry. None of these are blocking but each is a small, isolated, in-scope fix. Lint, typecheck, all 919 tests, and quality-gate scripts are currently green; these changes must keep them green.
  approach
    Land each finding as a single small PR. Order: independent fixes first (trim bug, upgrade exit code, knip.json hints), then the dead-code removal (which is the largest churn), then the release step at the end. Each step touches a narrow file set, ships with the new/updated tests that lock in the fix, and runs the full gate suite locally before close. No abstractions introduced, no module boundaries moved. If during dead-code removal any export turns out to actually be referenced from outside the project (extensions, scripts), keep it and note it in the close reason instead of forcing the removal.
  steps
    1. Fix sd update --title to trim before storing
    2. Make sd upgrade --check --json exit non-zero when update available
    3. Clean up stale knip.json hints
    4. Remove unused exports and types flagged by knip
    5. Release: run /release per .claude/commands/release.md
  acceptance
    1. `bun run lint`, `bun run typecheck`, and `bun test` all exit 0 after every step lands.
    2. `bunx knip` reports zero unused exports and zero unused exported types after the dead-code step.
    3. `bunx knip` reports zero configuration hints after the knip.json cleanup step.
    4. A new test asserts `sd update --title '  hello  '` stores `title === 'hello'` (matches `sd create` trim behavior).
    5. A new test asserts `sd upgrade --check --json` sets a non-zero process exit code when `current !== latest`, matching the non-JSON path.
  behavior_invariant
    All existing public CLI surfaces keep their current contracts. `sd update --title` still validates non-empty titles and rejects whitespace-only titles, exactly as today. `sd upgrade` (no --check) keeps its current behavior in both human and JSON modes. The `--json` envelope shape for every command stays as `{success, command, ...}` — no key renames, no removals from JSON payloads. `bun run lint`, `bun run typecheck`, `bun test`, `bun run check:size`, `bun run check:debt`, `bun run check:agents`, and `bun run check:coverage` all continue to exit 0. No public TypeScript signature changes, no new dependencies, no new CLI flags or subcommands.

Children (5):
  seeds-dfe7  [open]  Fix sd update --title to trim before storing
  seeds-15ae  [open]  Make sd upgrade --check --json exit non-zero when update available
  seeds-c437  [open]  Clean up stale knip.json hints
  seeds-faab  [open]  Remove unused exports and types flagged by knip
  seeds-f7ef  [open]  Release: run /release per .claude/commands/release.md
